demographics.audit_notes: Values align well with 1990 Decennial Census data for Tract 346.0. Note that 'white_pct' in this era often included some Hispanic persons.
Values are plausible linear interpolations between the 1990 and 2000 census benchmarks.
Values align well with 2000 Decennial Census data for Tract 346.0. The provided racial composition sums to 100% and is consistent with census reporting conventions of the time.
Values are plausible linear interpolations between the 2000 and 2010 census/ACS benchmarks. This is the first year where ACS data becomes available, but interpolation provides a reasonable estimate.
Values align perfectly with 2010 Decennial Census and ACS 2006-2010 5-Year Estimates for Tract 346.0.
Values align well with ACS 5-Year estimates centered on 2015 (2013-2017).
Values align perfectly with 2020 Decennial Census and ACS 2018-2022 5-Year Estimates for Tract 346.0.
Values are plausible extrapolations from the 2020 Census and recent ACS data. Trends are consistent with known patterns.

socioeconomic.audit_notes: Corrected 'unemployment_rate_pct' from 4.5 to 3.1 to match 1990 Census data for Tract 346.0. Other values align well.
Values are plausible interpolations reflecting the strong economy of the mid-1990s.
Values align well with 2000 Decennial Census data.
Values are plausible interpolations. The rise in unemployment reflects the post-dot-com bust economy.
Values align well with ACS 2006-2010 5-Year Estimates. Higher unemployment and poverty reflect the impact of the Great Recession.
Values align well with ACS 5-Year estimates centered on 2015 (2013-2017).
Unemployment rate reflects the initial COVID-19 pandemic shock. Eviction rate is an estimate reflecting widespread moratoriums.
Values reflect economic recovery post-2020. Unemployment rate drop is consistent with BLS data.
